Class AsyncScanBuilder<T>

java.lang.Object
com.hogwai.dynamodb.simplified.async.AsyncScanBuilder<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the type of the item
public class AsyncScanBuilder<T> extends Object
A fluent async builder for scanning a DynamoDB table with optional filter, projection, and pagination.

Mirrors the sync ScanBuilder but returns CompletableFuture from all execution methods and uses the AWS SDK's async PagePublisher under the hood.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    AsyncScanBuilder(@NonNull software.amazon.awssdk.enhanced.dynamodb.DynamoDbAsyncIndex<T> index)
    Constructs a new AsyncScanBuilder for scanning the given async secondary index.
    AsyncScanBuilder(@NonNull software.amazon.awssdk.enhanced.dynamodb.DynamoDbAsyncIndex<T> index, @Nullable software.amazon.awssdk.services.dynamodb.DynamoDbAsyncClient dynamoDbAsyncClient)
    Constructs a new AsyncScanBuilder for the given async index with a low-level client.
    AsyncScanBuilder(@NonNull software.amazon.awssdk.enhanced.dynamodb.DynamoDbAsyncTable<T> table)
    Constructs a new AsyncScanBuilder for the given async table.

    Constructs a new AsyncScanBuilder for the given async table with a low-level client.

    @NonNull AsyncScanBuilder<T>
    consistentRead(boolean consistentRead)
    Enables or disables strongly consistent reads.

    Returns the total number of items asynchronously by iterating all scan result pages.

    Executes the scan asynchronously and returns all matching items aggregated from all pages.

    Executes the scan asynchronously and returns the first matching item, if any.

    Returns a reactive publisher that lazily emits items as pages arrive.

    executeWithPagination()
    Executes the scan asynchronously and returns only the first page of results along with the last evaluated key for pagination.

    • AsyncScanBuilder

      public AsyncScanBuilder(@NonNull software.amazon.awssdk.enhanced.dynamodb.DynamoDbAsyncTable<T> table)
      Constructs a new AsyncScanBuilder for the given async table.
      Parameters:
      table - the async DynamoDB table

    • AsyncScanBuilder

      public AsyncScanBuilder(@NonNull software.amazon.awssdk.enhanced.dynamodb.DynamoDbAsyncIndex<T> index)
      Constructs a new AsyncScanBuilder for scanning the given async secondary index.
      Parameters:
      index - the async DynamoDB secondary index

    • AsyncScanBuilder

      public AsyncScanBuilder(@NonNull software.amazon.awssdk.enhanced.dynamodb.DynamoDbAsyncTable<T> table, @Nullable software.amazon.awssdk.services.dynamodb.DynamoDbAsyncClient dynamoDbAsyncClient)

      Parameters:

      dynamoDbAsyncClient - the low-level async DynamoDB client (nullable)

  • Method Details

    • filter

      public @NonNull AsyncScanBuilder<T> filter(@NonNull Consumer<FilterExpression> filterBuilder)
      Configures a scan filter expression using a FilterExpression consumer.
      Parameters:
      filterBuilder - a consumer that configures the FilterExpression
      Returns:
      this builder for chaining

    • filter

      public @NonNull AsyncScanBuilder<T> filter(@Nullable FilterExpression filter)
      Configures a scan filter expression from a pre-built FilterExpression.
      Parameters:
      filter - the filter expression
      Returns:
      this builder for chaining

    • filter

      public @NonNull AsyncScanBuilder<T> filter(@NonNull Map<String,Object> conditions)
      Configures a scan filter from a map of attribute-value pairs.

      All conditions are combined with AND. Each entry is treated as an equality filter. For other condition types, use filter(Consumer).

      Parameters:
      conditions - a map of attribute names to their expected values
      Returns:
      this builder for chaining

    • project

      public @NonNull AsyncScanBuilder<T> project(@NonNull String... attributes)
      Restricts the returned attributes to the specified ones.
      Parameters:
      attributes - the attribute names to include in the result
      Returns:
      this builder for chaining

    • project

      public @NonNull AsyncScanBuilder<T> project(@NonNull Consumer<ProjectionExpression> projectionBuilder)
      Restricts the returned attributes by configuring a ProjectionExpression via a consumer.
      Parameters:
      projectionBuilder - a consumer that configures the ProjectionExpression
      Returns:
      this builder for chaining

    • parallelScan

      public @NonNull AsyncScanBuilder<T> parallelScan(int totalSegments, int segment)
      Configures a parallel scan by specifying the total number of segments and the segment index for this scan worker.
      Parameters:
      totalSegments - the total number of segments to divide the table into
      segment - the segment index for this worker (0-based)
      Returns:
      this builder for chaining

    • limit

      public @NonNull AsyncScanBuilder<T> limit(int limit)
      Limits the number of items evaluated per page.
      Parameters:
      limit - the maximum number of items to evaluate per page
      Returns:
      this builder for chaining

    • startFrom

      public @NonNull AsyncScanBuilder<T> startFrom(@Nullable Map<String, software.amazon.awssdk.services.dynamodb.model.AttributeValue> lastEvaluatedKey)
      Sets the exclusive start key for paginated scans. Typically obtained from the PagedResult.lastEvaluatedKey() of a previous scan.
      Parameters:
      lastEvaluatedKey - the key map from which to start the next page
      Returns:
      this builder for chaining

    • consistentRead

      public @NonNull AsyncScanBuilder<T> consistentRead(boolean consistentRead)
      Enables or disables strongly consistent reads.
      Parameters:
      consistentRead - true for a strongly consistent read, false (the default) for an eventually consistent read
      Returns:
      this builder for chaining

    • returnConsumedCapacity

      public @NonNull AsyncScanBuilder<T> returnConsumedCapacity(@NonNull software.amazon.awssdk.services.dynamodb.model.ReturnConsumedCapacity returnConsumedCapacity)
      Configures whether to return the consumed capacity for the scan operation.
      Parameters:
      returnConsumedCapacity - the ReturnConsumedCapacity value
      Returns:
      this builder for chaining

    • select

      public @NonNull AsyncScanBuilder<T> select(@Nullable software.amazon.awssdk.services.dynamodb.model.Select select)
      Sets the select parameter for the scan, controlling which attributes are returned. Use Select.COUNT to request only the item count from the server.
      Parameters:
      select - the select parameter (nullable)
      Returns:
      this builder for chaining

    • executeAll

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<List<T>> executeAll()
      Executes the scan asynchronously and returns all matching items aggregated from all pages.

      Memory warning: This method collects all pages into a single in-memory list before returning. For scans that may return a large number of items, consider using executeWithPagination() or processing pages individually to avoid excessive memory usage.

      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture containing a list of matching items

    • executeWithPagination

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<PagedResult<T>> executeWithPagination()
      Executes the scan asynchronously and returns only the first page of results along with the last evaluated key for pagination.
      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture containing a PagedResult with the first page of items and the last evaluated key (may be null if no more pages)

    • executeAndGetFirst

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Optional<T>> executeAndGetFirst()
      Executes the scan asynchronously and returns the first matching item, if any.

      Only the first page of results is loaded: subsequent pages are never fetched.

      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture containing an Optional with the first item, or empty if no items match

    • executeStream

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<software.amazon.awssdk.core.async.SdkPublisher<T>> executeStream()
      Returns a reactive publisher that lazily emits items as pages arrive.

      Unlike executeAll() which loads all pages into memory, this method streams items one-by-one as each page is received from DynamoDB.

      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture containing an SdkPublisher that emits scan items
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if called with Select.COUNT

    • count

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Long> count()
      Returns the total number of items asynchronously by iterating all scan result pages.

      When a low-level DynamoDbAsyncClient is available, uses Select.COUNT server-side to avoid transferring item data. Falls back to the enhanced client page iteration otherwise.

      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture containing the total count of scanned items matching the filter
